items.md: the menehunes — the tiny ones. *(May 28, 1953)*
items.value: the menehunes — the tiny ones. (May 28, 1953)

items.md: According to Barks, his wife Garé — who had begun working as his assistant only shortly before — suggested the locale for this story, and told him about the menehunes. *(August 1978 letter to Michael Barrier)*
items.value: According to Barks, his wife Garé — who had begun working as his assistant only shortly before — suggested the locale for this story, and told him about the menehunes. (August 1978 letter to Michael Barrier)

items.md: UNCLE SCROOGE - 32 - Scrooge buys up all the 1916 quarters ever minted and sinks all but one deep in the Atlantic, with the aim of making that one coin the most
items.value: UNCLE SCROOGE - 32 - Scrooge buys up all the 1916 quarters ever minted and sinks all but one deep in the Atlantic, with the aim of making that one coin the most

items.md: valuable in the world. But the last quarter is flattened by a steamroller and so Scrooge must dive into the Atlantic to get another. There, the ducks find Atlantis, a sunken city populated by fish-men who breathe under water. *(July 30, 1953)*
items.value: valuable in the world. But the last quarter is flattened by a steamroller and so Scrooge must dive into the Atlantic to get another. There, the ducks find Atlantis, a sunken city populated by fish-men who breathe under water. (July 30, 1953)

items.md: Mentioned in this story is the "Junior Woodchucks' Book of Knowledge," the famous Junior Woodchuck Guidebook in embryo.

items.caption: A large comic panel showing a chaotic barroom brawl in the Klondike. Scrooge McDuck is in the center, swinging his fists and knocking down several large, bearded men. In the upper left, a speech bubble reads: "IT WAS A FIGHT TO BE PROUD OF! I WAS LIKE A BUZZSAW! MEN FELL IN ROWS LIKE DOMINOES!"
items.md: ![A large comic panel showing a chaotic barroom brawl in the Klondike. Scrooge McDuck is in the center, swinging his fists and knocking down several large, bearded men. In the upper left, a speech bubble reads: "IT WAS A FIGHT TO BE PROUD OF! I WAS LIKE A BUZZSAW! MEN FELL IN ROWS LIKE DOMINOES!"](CBatAotCB-134_page_1_image_1_v2.jpg)
This half-page panel was deleted from "Back to the Klondike" in *Uncle Scrooge* Four Color No. 456, 1953, before publication; © Walt Disney Productions.
